input.prompt:
I'm building a chat messaging screen in .NET MAUI targeting .NET 10. When the
keyboard opens, it covers my text input at the bottom of the screen. In .NET 9
I used WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize but that doesn't seem to work anymore.
How do I make the input bar move up when the keyboard appears?

input.initialPrompt:
I'm building a chat messaging screen in .NET MAUI targeting .NET 10. When the
keyboard opens, it covers my text input at the bottom of the screen. In .NET 9
I used WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize but that doesn't seem to work anymore.
How do I make the input bar move up when the keyboard appears?

data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about a .NET MAUI issue where the keyboard covers the text input at the bottom of the screen on Android. They mention that `WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize` worked in .NET 9 but doesn't work in .NET 10.

This is a known change in .NET MAUI / Android. In Android 11+ (API 30+), the `SOFT_INPUT_ADJUST_RESIZE` mode was deprecated, and in .NET MAUI 10 (targeting .NET 10), the framework moved toward using the newer `WindowInsets` API and edge-to-edge rendering.


The best approach in .NET 10 is to use the `KeyboardAutoManagerScroll` behavior or properly configure edge-to-edge insets, since the older `WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize` approach no longer works with the new Android insets system that MAUI has adopted. proper inset handling.

For .NET MAUI 10 (.NET 10), the key changes are:
- The app likely runs in edge-to-edge mode by default
- You need to use `android:windowSoftInputMode="adjustResize"` in the AndroidManifest AND handle window insets properly
- Or use MAUI's built-in `KeyboardAutoManagerScroll` which was introduced to handle this scenario

The most practical solutions are using `KeyboardAutoManagerScroll` for automatic keyboard scrolling, setting `android:windowSoftInputMode="adjustResize"` with proper inset handling, or manually adjusting padding based on keyboard visibility through the insets API. Since edge-to-edge is now the default in .NET 10, the key is ensuring your layout responds to window insets—particularly the IME inset—either through MAUI's built-in keyboard behavior or by listening to inset changes and adding bottom padding accordingly.
